 {"id":"bd-1yk8","title":"Data model: event annotations + triage state","description":"## What
Extend storage schema for event notes, labels, and triage state.

## Why
Without persistence, annotations are lost and triage workflows stay manual.

## How
- Add columns: triage_state, triage_updated_at, triage_updated_by
- Add separate table for labels/notes if needed for many-to-one
- Migrations + indexes for label/state filters

## Risks
- Schema migration must preserve existing events

## Success Criteria
- Storage APIs support add/update/read annotations and labels
- Indexes keep label/state filters fast","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:03:12.890296493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:43.693414775Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yk8","depends_on_id":"bd-2sua","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:03:12.897822459Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yk8","depends_on_id":"wa-y6g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:43.693383567Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1yzi","title":"E2E: saved searches (create/run/alert)","description":"## Scenarios
- Create saved search, list, run manually
- Scheduled run emits alert event
- Disable search prevents alerts

## Logging
- Capture CLI output JSON and timestamps
- Capture notification payloads with redaction

## Success Criteria
- E2E script produces artifacts with deterministic run order","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:13.101501340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:40.893587748Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yzi","depends_on_id":"bd-1t2b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:13.116229539Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yzi","depends_on_id":"bd-37f9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:37.343730378Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yzi","depends_on_id":"bd-4enj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:40.893537414Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1zgb","title":"E2E: reliability hardening scenarios with verbose logs","description":"## Goal
Validate resilience features end-to-end with detailed logging and artifacts.

## Requirements
- Execute fault injection scenarios that trigger circuit breaker, retry, and graceful degradation paths.
- Capture verbose logs (trace/debug) plus key metrics snapshots as artifacts.
- Verify user-facing output is stable and actionable during degraded mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E scenarios pass locally and in CI.
- Failure artifacts include full logs, triggered fault labels, and observed policy outputs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:24.619323361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:47.826611461Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"wa-37x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-20fw","title":"Create WezTerm fork with wa feature flag and minimal integration points","description":"## Overview

Fork WezTerm and add the minimal code needed to support wa event delivery. All changes are behind \`#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]\`.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: bd-3gon (WaEventSink trait design)

## Repository Setup

### 1. Fork WezTerm
\`\`\`bash
gh repo fork wez/wezterm --clone
cd wezterm
git checkout -b feature/wa-integration
\`\`\`

### 2. Add Feature Flag
In Cargo.toml (workspace root):
\`\`\`toml
[workspace.features]
wa-integration = []
\`\`\`

In relevant crates (mux/Cargo.toml, wezterm/Cargo.toml):
\`\`\`toml
[features]
wa-integration = []
\`\`\`

## Modification Points

### 1. mux/src/lib.rs — Event Sink Registration

\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
mod wa_events;

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
use wa_events::WaEventSink;

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
static WA_EVENT_SINK: OnceCell<Arc<dyn WaEventSink>> = OnceCell::new();

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
pub fn register_wa_event_sink(sink: Arc<dyn WaEventSink>) {
    WA_EVENT_SINK.set(sink).ok();
}

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
pub(crate) fn emit_wa_event(event: impl FnOnce(&dyn WaEventSink)) {
    if let Some(sink) = WA_EVENT_SINK.get() {
        event(sink.as_ref());
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit Output Events

In the method that receives PTY output:
\`\`\`rust
fn process_pty_output(&mut self, data: &[u8]) {
    // Existing WezTerm code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_pane_output(self.pane_id, data);
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 3. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit State Changes

In methods that update pane state:
\`\`\`rust
fn set_title(&mut self, title: String) {
    // Existing code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    self.emit_state_change();
}

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
fn emit_state_change(&self) {
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_pane_state_change(self.pane_id, &self.get_wa_state());
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 4. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit User-Var Changes

In the OSC 1337 handler:
\`\`\`rust
fn set_user_var(&mut self, name: String, value: String) {
    // Existing code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_user_var_changed(self.pane_id, &name, &value);
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 5. wezterm/src/main.rs — Initialize Sink

\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
fn init_wa_integration() {
    if let Ok(socket_path) = std::env::var("WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET") {
        match wa_socket_sink::connect(&socket_path) {
            Ok(sink) => mux::register_wa_event_sink(sink),
            Err(e) => log::warn!("Failed to connect to wa socket: {}", e),
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Testing the Fork

### 1. Build with Feature
\`\`\`bash
cargo build --features wa-integration
\`\`\`

### 2. Verify Default Build Unaffected
\`\`\`bash
cargo build  # Should work identically to upstream
\`\`\`

### 3. Integration Test
\`\`\`bash
# Start wa watch (which creates the socket)
wa watch &

# Start WezTerm with wa integration
WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET=/tmp/wa/events.sock ./target/debug/wezterm

# Verify events received
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] WezTerm fork created with feature/wa-integration branch
- [ ] Feature flag added to workspace
- [ ] Event emission added to pane output path
- [ ] Event emission added to state change paths
- [ ] Event emission added to user-var handler
- [ ] Default build (no feature) compiles and runs unchanged
- [ ] Feature build compiles and runs
- [ ] Events actually received by wa (integration test)

## Risks

- WezTerm internal APIs may change — minimize touchpoints
- Performance regression — benchmark before/after
- Thread safety bugs — careful with event emission

## Files to Modify (in WezTerm fork)

- Cargo.toml (workspace) — add feature
- mux/Cargo.toml — add feature
- mux/src/lib.rs — event sink registration
- mux/src/pane.rs — event emission
- wezterm/Cargo.toml — add feature
- wezterm/src/main.rs — initialization
- NEW: mux/src/wa_events.rs — trait + socket sink implementation

## References

- WezTerm mux: https://github.com/wez/wezterm/tree/main/mux
- Rust feature flags: https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/features.html","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperLantern","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:14.103113431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T04:33:57.089375881Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20fw","depends_on_id":"bd-2xe4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:14.123064266Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-20fw","depends_on_id":"bd-3gon","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:24.240909832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":179,"issue_id":"bd-20fw","author":"CopperLantern","text":"Added [workspace.features] wa-integration = [] to /data/projects/wezterm/Cargo.toml on feature/wa-integration. Existing commits already add mux event sink + per-crate feature wiring. Build succeeded. Warnings: unused manifest key workspace.features in root Cargo.toml, and an upstream unused_assignments warning in wezterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/screen_line.rs:677.","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:32:52Z"},{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-20fw","author":"CopperLantern","text":"Also ran cargo build (default) in /data/projects/wezterm. Build succeeded; same warnings: workspace.features unused manifest key, and upstream unused_assignments warning in wezterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/screen_line.rs:677.","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:33:57Z"}]}

[features]

### 1. mux/src/lib.rs — Event Sink Registration

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
pub fn register_wa_event_sink(sink: Arc<dyn WaEventSink>) {
    WA_EVENT_SINK.set(sink).ok();

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
pub(crate) fn emit_wa_event(event: impl FnOnce(&dyn WaEventSink)) {

### 2. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit Output Events

In the method that receives PTY output:

In methods that update pane state:

\`\`\`

## Testing the Fork

### 3. Integration Test

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Event emission added to pane output path
- [ ] Event emission added to state change paths

- [ ] Default build (no feature) compiles and runs unchanged
- [ ] Feature build compiles and runs

## Risks

- WezTerm internal APIs may change — minimize touchpoints
- Performance regression — benchmark before/after
- Thread safety bugs — careful with event emission

To validate listener end-to-end, sent synthetic native events over the socket (pane_created + pane_output) and verified persistence via sqlite: pane_id=999 exists in panes, output_segments contains "hello from native". `wa robot state` hung due to wezterm CLI list timeouts (no GUI), circuit breaker opened in watcher logs.  {"id":"bd-21ml","title":"Benchmark WezTerm performance before/after STATUS_UPDATE_LUA removal","description":"## Overview

Measure WezTerm responsiveness before and after removing STATUS_UPDATE_LUA to quantify the performance improvement. This validates the optimization and provides data for the decision to pursue (or skip) Phase 2/3.

## Methodology

### 1. Baseline Measurement (Before)

With STATUS_UPDATE_LUA active:

\`\`\`bash
# Terminal responsiveness test
time for i in {1..1000}; do echo "line $i"; done

# Scrollback stress test
seq 1 10000 | while read n; do echo "Stress test line $n with some padding text to fill the line"; done

# Interactive typing latency (subjective)
# Type rapidly and observe lag
\`\`\`

### 2. Measurement (After)

Same tests with STATUS_UPDATE_LUA removed.

### 3. Metrics to Capture

| Metric | How to Measure |
|--------|----------------|
| Echo throughput | Lines/second in echo loop |
| Scroll smoothness | Subjective + frame timing |
| Input latency | Time from keypress to echo (harder to measure) |
| CPU usage | \`htop\` or \`perf\` during tests |
| WezTerm frame rate | If profiling tools available |

### 4. Test Scenarios

1. **Idle pane**: Just cursor blinking — update-status fires constantly
2. **Active output**: \`yes\` or \`seq\` flooding terminal
3. **Multiple panes**: 4+ panes, some active
4. **With wa watch running**: Baseline vs optimized
5. **Without wa watch**: Pure WezTerm baseline

## Tools

### CPU Profiling
\`\`\`bash
# Linux perf
perf record -g -p $(pgrep wezterm) -- sleep 10
perf report

# macOS Instruments
instruments -t "Time Profiler" -p $(pgrep wezterm) -D profile.trace
\`\`\`

### Frame Timing
WezTerm may have debug options for frame timing. Check config:
\`\`\`lua
config.debug_key_events = true
-- Other debug options
\`\`\`

### Memory
\`\`\`bash
# Watch WezTerm memory usage
while true; do ps -o rss= -p $(pgrep wezterm); sleep 1; done
\`\`\`

## Expected Results

| Scenario | Before (Lua) | After (No Lua) | Improvement |
|----------|--------------|----------------|-------------|
| Idle CPU | ~5-15% | ~1-3% | 3-5x |
| Echo throughput | X lines/s | Y lines/s | TBD |
| Typing latency | Perceptible lag | No lag | Subjective |

## Recording Results

Document results in:
1. This bead's comments (use \`br comments add\`)
2. PR description when merging
3. CHANGELOG.md under "Performance" section

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Baseline measurements recorded (before)
- [ ] Post-optimization measurements recorded (after)
- [ ] Improvement quantified (percentage or ratio)
- [ ] Results documented
- [ ] User confirms subjective improvement
- [ ] Decision: proceed to Phase 2/3 or not?

## Dependencies

- Should be done: After bd-30gj (STATUS_UPDATE_LUA removed)
- Before: bd-mwst (documentation) — to include benchmark results

## Files

- Create: benchmarks/lua_removal_results.md (or add to docs/)

## Notes

This is a **validation task**. If results don't show improvement, we need to investigate why (maybe the bottleneck is elsewhere).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:05.693319255Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:09:13.713378695Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:09:13.713234457Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21ml","depends_on_id":"bd-2l5s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:05.709998681Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-21ml","depends_on_id":"bd-30gj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:15.720326696Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":76,"issue_id":"bd-21ml","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Benchmark Status

**Note**: Baseline measurements (before STATUS_UPDATE_LUA removal) were not captured before the optimization was implemented. The code was removed as part of bd-30gj and bd-2z6d.

### Theoretical Improvement

The `update-status` Lua event was firing at ~60Hz (every frame), which caused:
- Continuous Lua interpreter invocations
- Repeated JSON serialization of pane state
- IPC overhead for each status update

With STATUS_UPDATE_LUA removed:
- Alt-screen detection now uses escape sequence parsing (zero Lua overhead)
- Pane metadata obtained from `wezterm cli list` only when needed
- No continuous polling or event firing

### Recommendation

Close this task and document the change in bd-mwst. User can validate subjective improvement by testing WezTerm responsiveness with/without `wa watch` running.

### Files Changed (for reference)
- Removed STATUS_UPDATE_LUA from setup.rs
- Removed StatusUpdate types/handlers from ipc.rs  
- Removed StatusUpdateReceived event from events.rs
- Created screen_state.rs for escape sequence-based alt-screen detection
","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:08:57Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3hoe","title":"FTS maintenance: verify/rebuild command","description":"## What
Provide a CLI command to verify and rebuild FTS indexes with progress.

## Why
Users need a safe recovery path when search results are inconsistent.

## How
- `wa search fts verify` checks row counts and index integrity
- `wa search fts rebuild` runs a rebuild with progress output

## Success Criteria
- Commands are safe and idempotent
- Progress is visible and redacted","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:09:54.401466312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:09:54.408157242Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hoe","depends_on_id":"bd-2i2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:09:54.408113921Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3kzu","title":"TUI/Web surfaces for saved searches","description":"## What
Expose saved searches in TUI and web dashboards.

## Why
Operators should be able to view/trigger saved searches without memorizing CLI flags.

## How
- TUI: list saved searches, run on demand, toggle enabled
- Web: read-only list + last run status (no mutations yet)

## Risks
- Feature gating: keep behind tui/web feature flags

## Success Criteria
- Saved searches visible in TUI and web
- No direct DB reads from UI; use shared query client","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:54.854781244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:16:37.424847534Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"bd-1t2b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:54.864273971Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"bd-37f9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:30.257461340Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"bd-4enj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:33.800434310Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:16:37.424812299Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:16:35.220851055Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3lai","title":"Config schema: pane priorities + budgets","description":"## What
Define config fields for per-pane priorities and capture budgets.

## Why
Operators need explicit, documented knobs to tune capture behavior.

## How
- Add config entries for default priority + per-pane overrides
- Add budget settings (max captures/sec, max bytes/sec)
- Validation + defaults for backward compatibility

## Success Criteria
- Config validates and loads with sensible defaults
- Documentation includes examples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:04:37.625306400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T02:59:39.726435103Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T02:59:39.726360123Z","close_reason":"Added ingest priorities + capture budgets config schema, validation, tests, README example","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lai","depends_on_id":"bd-396b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:04:37.643654756Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3m3a","title":"E2E: CLI polish validation with verbose logs","description":"## Goal
Validate shell completions, aliases, and progressive disclosure end-to-end with detailed logging.

## Requirements
- Exercise completions and aliases in a scripted shell session with verbose logs enabled.
- Verify help output and verbosity tiers are stable (default/verbose/debug).
- Capture logs, command outputs, and completion snapshots as artifacts on failure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E CLI polish scenario passes locally and in CI.
- Failure artifacts include full logs, completion outputs, and help snapshots.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:47.793107080Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:18.766547718Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:18.766490961Z","close_reason":"Created scripts/e2e_cli_polish.sh - comprehensive E2E tests for CLI polish features.
